Platform — Developer Experience

API-first. Every capability, accessible by code.

Cinturon360 is built API-first — every platform capability is accessible via the REST API. TMC integrations, corporate system connections, and custom workflow extensions all use the same API that powers the platform itself.

🔌
REST API

Full platform access

Bookings, approvals, policy rules, reporting queries, user management, and integration configuration — all available via a consistent REST API with standard authentication.

📄
Documentation

Developer documentation

API reference, integration guides, and code examples. Documentation is versioned alongside the API — you'll always know what changed and when.

🔐
Auth & Access

Secure by design

OAuth 2.0 authentication, API key management, rate limiting, and per-tenant access controls. TMC and corporate system integrations get scoped access, not blanket permissions.

What developers use the API for

TMC system integrations

Connect proprietary TMC booking tools or back-office systems to the Cinturon360 governance and reporting layer.

Corporate ERP connections

Feed travel approval and spend data directly into SAP, Oracle, or other ERP systems via structured API output.

Custom workflow triggers

Trigger external actions on approval events — Slack notifications, internal ticketing systems, finance platform webhooks.

Reporting data pipelines

Pull structured travel spend and compliance data into BI tools, data warehouses, or custom finance dashboards.

API documentation coming soon

Full API reference and developer documentation are in progress and will be published alongside platform access. Early access partners and design partners can request API documentation directly.

See the API overview →

Building on top of Cinturon360?

Talk to the team about early API access and integration partnerships for TMCs and developers.
